nameMeanings.name: Farley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Farley, of which there are examples in Berkshire, Derbyshire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Gloucestershire, Staffordshire, Surrey, Wiltshire, Shropshire, and Sussex. From Old English fearn ‘fern’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. See also Farleigh , Fairley , Fairlie .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fearghaile (see Farrelly ).

variants.name: Farleigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Farleigh, of which there are examples in Hampshire, Devon, Kent, Somerset, Surrey, and Wiltshire. From Old English fearn ‘fern’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. See also Farley , Fairley , Fairlie .

variants.name: Farlow
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from Old English fearn ‘fern’ + hlāw ‘hill, tumulus’. This surname may have become confused with Farley .

variants.name: Fearnley
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from any of various places called with Old English fearn ‘fern, bracken’ + lēah ‘wood, clearing’, notably the Yorkshire places Farnley near Otley, Farnley near Armley, Farnley Tyas, Fearnley in Wyke, Fernley Hill in Altofts, and perhaps also Fernilee in Derbyshire.

variants.name: Farlee
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Farley  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard Farley was born in 1554, in Ware, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. He married Elizabeth in 1574, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Farley est né en 1554, à Ware, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Elizabeth en 1574, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Farley wurde 1554 in Ware, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat 1574 in England Elizabeth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Farley è nato il nel 1554 nel Ware, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Elizabeth nel 1574, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Farley(은)는 1554년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 워에서 태어났다. 그는 1574년 영국에서 Elizabeth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Farley родился в 1554 г. в местности вар, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth в 1574 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Farley nació en 1554, en Ware, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Elizabeth en 1574, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
